arts.ttl: West Brom is the biggest match of the season so far, admits Kasey Palmer
arts.sum: City are without a win in their last six games, with three draws and three defeats in that run, the last of which came at Oxford United on Tuesday night, when Tim Walter was jeered by the away supporters, having seen his team booed off last weekend against bottom side Portsmouth. Sunday's home meeting with West Brom is the biggest game so far of this fledgling Championship season, according to Hull City's Kasey Palmer.

arts.ttl: Tim Walter issues passionate rallying cry to Hull City players as West Brom test looms
arts.sum: Walter says fans must realise the squad is not at the same level as last season, which saw Liam Rosenior able to call upon the likes of Jacob Greaves, Jean Michael Seri, Tyler Morton, Jaden Philogene, Liam Delap and Fabio Carvalho. "Maybe we are not as far as the team was last year because you had players like Philogene, Greaves, Delap, and Carvalho," he said.

arts.ttl: Hull City have five games to give suffering supporters reason to be positive
arts.sum: Only Burton Albion and Carlisle United have worse records at home so far in the calendar year and, as it stands, both of those are heading for relegation from League One and Two respectively. Watching City at home this year has been, for the most part, a chore.

arts.ttl: Preview: Hull City vs. West Bromwich Albion - prediction, team news, lineups
arts.sum: Sports Mole previews Sunday's Championship clash between Hull City and West Bromwich Albion, including predictions, team news and possible lineups.

arts.ttl: ‘Close to promotion’ – Hull City boss Tim Walter has say on ‘experienced’ West Brom side
arts.sum: West Brom have been labelled as a ‘really experienced’ side by Hull City boss Tim Walter. West Brom… The post ‘Close to promotion’ – Hull City boss Tim Walter has say on ‘experienced’ West Brom side appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: Hull City left reeling after Mohamed Belloumi injury bombshell on the eve of pivotal WBA duel
arts.sum: " Hull City can confirm that winger Mohamed Belloumi has suffered an injury to his anterior cruciate ligament, sustained during Tuesday night’s fixture with Oxford United and as such is likely to miss the remainder of the season," the club said in a statement on Friday afternoon. Mohamed Belloumi ruptured his anterior cruciate ligament in his knee late on in Tuesday night’s 1-0 defeat at Oxford United and will be sidelined for the rest of the season after scans confirmed the news the club were expecting.
